{{Quote|Subject is an escaped convict. Armed and dangerous|Innk's bounty posting by the Republic Correctional Authority|<ref name="BH" />}}
'''Yo-Hann Innk''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kadas'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kadas'sa'Nikto]] who was active during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] was imprisoned but managed to escape his confinement and fled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] where he came to be affiliated with [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla Besadii the Elder]]. The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] captured Innk on Tatooine, claiming a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on him by the [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Republic Correctional Authority]].

==Biography==
By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Yo-Hann Innk had been previously incarcerated but managed to escape his imprisonment, leading to the [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Republic Correctional Authority]] placing a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on the convicts head. The Authority labeled him armed and dangerous and offer to pay 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture alive but only 1,000 credits if he was [[Death/Legends|Dead]]. After his escape Yo-Hann made his way to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] where he became affiliated with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla Besadii the Elder]]. In 32 BBY the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] traveled to Tatooine in order to track down a Hutt affiliated with [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], a cult lead by the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Komari Vosa]] on whom a bounty had been placed which Fett meant to claim. [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], another Hutt crime lord and a rival of Gardulla, informed the hunter that Gardulla was the Hutt he sought and so the Mandalorian traveled to her [[Imperial Station Bravo|palace]] via the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]].<ref name="BH" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
Whilst traveling in the Dune Sea Fett encountered Yo-Hann, who was traveling on board a [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|''Bantha''-II cargo skiff]] and fighting the native [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] amongst the desert canyons. Fett used his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to reach Innk's vehicle and then subdued the Nikto capturing him alive and claiming the bounty of 2000 credits.<ref name="BH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Innk was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] [[Subspecies/Legends|Subspecies]] known as the [[Kadas'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kadas'sa'Nikto]],<ref name="BH" /> who normally had [[Color/Legends|green]] colored skin,<ref name="GG12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref> although unusually he possessed purple colored skin. The convict had red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and was hairless over his entire body.<ref name="BH" />

==Equipment==
The Nikto choose to use a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] in combat and wore [[Armor/Legends|armor]] on his torso. During whilst in the Dune Sea he rode on a ''Bantha''-2 cargo skiff from which he fired down on Tusken Raiders.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yo-Hann Innk first appeared in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' released in [[December]] [[2002]] by [[LucasArts]]. Innk appears in the game in the level titled "Tusken Canyon", the second level to take place on Tatooine which is the fifth setting visited in the game. Innk's role is that of a secondary bounty, part of an optional objective found on each level which lets the player capture some of the enemies they fight to unlock bonus content. The Nikto is one of fifteen such bounties on the "Tusken canyon" level and is the fourth one the player will encounter. Players have several options upon meeting Innk being able to capture him alive, claim the bounty on him dead or simply kill him without claiming the bounty at all. Each of the possibilities is optional in terms of completing both the level and the game but this article assumes that the player captures Innk alive as this rewards the player the most [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].
